name: sonarqube
explanation: SonarQube has two distinct collection modes: (1) a web API instance using `web_endpoint` (typically http://host:9000) plus basic auth, and (2) a JMX instance using JMXFetch with multiple JVMs (web, ce, search) on user-defined ports. The web API requires credentials in any non-trivial deployment, and the README confirms `username`/`password` are needed. The JMX side requires host+port+user+password and the ports are not standardized (10443/10444/10445 are documentation examples). Either path requires credentials, so auto-config cannot complete from the wire.
notes: Dual-mode (HTTP API + JMX) where both modes need credentials. Even the unauthenticated web case requires the user to supply `components` (or `components_discovery`) to know what to scrape — the integration will not collect anything without it.
